methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $148M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Nearfield Instruments is a Series C semiconductor company founded by Roland van Vliet and Hamed Sadeghian that has raised $147.6M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a universe of 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers in chips and semiconductors, including same-stage reference points such as DEEPX ($529M, Series C) and later-stage companies like SambaNova Systems ($5.0B, Series D) and Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D).

Two methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $148M total raised proxy, implying a $251M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 4-company comparable set yields a $1.9B implied value, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while the semiconductor sector shows a 20.6x median price-to-sales multiple across the three public tickers, Nearfield's revenue is not disclosed.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $213M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide spread reflects the early-stage nature of the peer set and substantial dispersion in later-stage semiconductor company valuations. Key uncertainties include target revenue and gross margins relative to peers, and whether the company's technical approach and market timing align more closely with earlier-stage or later-stage comparables.
